summary: Dogecoin has no token vesting schedules, investor allocations, team tranches, or time-locked unlocks of any kind. It launched in December 2013 as a fair-launch proof-of-work meme coin with no pre-mine, no ICO, no founder allocation, and no foundation reserve. New DOGE enters circulation solely through block rewards paid to miners: the emission was capped at a fixed 10,000 DOGE per block (after several halving events in its early years) and has been permanently inflationary at ~5.256 billion DOGE per year since block 600,000 (February 2015), with no hard supply cap. There is therefore zero 'unlock pressure' in the traditional venture/token-sale sense; the only supply-side pressure is the continuous, predictable miner emission.
caveats: tokenunlocks.app, CryptoRank.io, and Messari do not list any vesting events for DOGE because none exist — these platforms confirm the fair-launch / pure-PoW nature of the project. The annual emission figure (~5.256 billion DOGE/yr, ~10,000 DOGE per ~1-minute block) is derived from the Dogecoin core protocol rules documented at https://github.com/dogecoin/dogecoin and summarised at https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/dogecoin and https://messari.io/asset/dogecoin/profile. No private-sale, team, or foundation unlock data exists because no such allocations were ever created.
